title: Study on the Correlation Between the TCM Syndrome Element and Microalbunminuria in Metabolic Syndrome
abstract: Objective: To investigate the relationships of the TCM syndrome element and microalbuminuria in metabolic syndrome(MS).Methods: Collected four diagnostic data of 340 cases of metabolic syndrome,adopt 'syndrome differentiation based on syndrome element' method to analysis the diagnostic informations by using statistic process,and detected microalbuminuria at the same time.Results: ①The common elements of metabolic syndrome: qi deficiency,blood deficiency,yin deficiency,yang deficiency,depletion of fluid,phlegm,blood stasis,dampness,heat,qi stagnation,heart,liver,spleen,lung and kidney.②Stepwise analysis showed that microalbum level was associated positively with kidney,yang deficiency and blood stasis.Standard regression coefficient were 0.47(P0.01),0.42(P0.01),0.334(P0.05).Conclusion: It can speculate that microalbunminuria can be used as an objective indexe of MS with kidney and yang deficiency.

title: Study on Characteristics of TCM Syndromes of Metabolic Syndrome
abstract: ObjectiveTo discuss the characteristics of TCM syndromes of metabolic syndrome (MS) and syndrome combination rules by analyzing symptoms of MS and TCM syndromes; To provide references for clinical treatment.Methods 200 patients with MS were chosen. Epidemiological investigation method was used. Standard case questionnaire was filled in objectively. The data were used to establish database. Statistical method was used to analyze characteristics of distribution, TCM syndrome distribution, and syndrome combination rules of MS.Results There were 16 common symptoms of MS, such as fatigue and thirst; frequency of 9 common symptoms in descending order was qi-deficiency syndrome, phlegm-damp syndrome, blood-stagnation syndrome, yin-deficiency syndrome, damp-heat syndrome, dryness-heat syndrome, kidney-deficiency syndrome, qi-depression syndrome, and yang-deficiency syndrome; two-syndrome combinations occupied 50% of all compound syndromes, among which excessive syndromes were mainly manifested as phlegm-damp syndrome + blood-stagnation syndrome, and deficiency syndromes were mainly manifested as qi-deficiency syndrome + yin-deficiency syndrome. Three-syndrome combinations occupied 50% of all compound syndromes, which were all syndromes of intermingled deficiency and excess.Conclusion MS mainly consists of compound syndromes. High-density lipoprotein cholesterol level of blood-stagnation syndrome is much higher than that of yin-deficiency syndrome and dryness-heat syndrome (P<0.05).

title: Research of the Objectification of Traditional Chinese Medical Syndrome Differentiation of Metabolism Syndrome
abstract: Objective:To explore the objective parameters for traditional Chinese medi- cal syndrome differentiation of metabolism syndrome.Methods:One hundred patients with metabolism syndrome were enrolled into this study.The parameters of insulin resistance in- dex(ISI),high-density lipoprotein cholesterol(HDL-C),total glyceride(TG),high sensitive C-reactive protein(hs-CRP)and tumor necrosis factorα(TNF-α)were de- tected.The relationship of syndrome patterns with the above parameters was analyzed with SPSS 10.0 software.Results:There were mainly six syndrome patterns for metabolism syn- drome:blockage of phlegm-turbidity,phlegm blended with blood stasis,deficiency of both Qi and yin.excessive heat due to yin deficiency,hyperactivity of liver yang.and deficiency of both yin and yang.The commonly-seen syndrome patterns reflected the pathophysiologi- col changes of metabolism syndrome.Conclusion:The levels of TG,ISI,hs-CRP and TNF-αare different in the six syndrome patterns,indicating that they can be used as the reference parameters for objectification of traditional Chinese medical syndrome differentiation of metabolism syndrome.

title: Study on the Relationships between TCM Constitution Types and Related Metabolic Indexes of Patients with Metabolic Syndrome
abstract: Objective:To study the relationships between TCM constitution types and related metabolic indexes of patients with metabolic syndrome,in order to provide an objective basis for TCM constitution interventions,improvement of biased constitution,and recovery of the health of individuals.Methods:By means of a cross-sectional study to collect of data and indexes of patients with metabolic syndrome;in accordance with "TCM Constitution Scale" to judge the constitution types and observe their distributions;by descriptive analysis,Logistic regression analysis etc.statistical methods,to analyze the relationships between the distribution law of TCM constitution and its related metabolic indexes.Results:Biased constitutions were in majority,the order of biased constitutions were respectively complex constitution,phlegmdamp,damp-heat,blood-stasis,qi-deficiency,yang- insufficiency,qi-stagnation,yin-deficiency,special innate.qi-stagnation,yin-deficiency,special innate.Among them,in complex constitution,phlegm-damp with blood-stasis was taken as main.The results of Logistic regression analysis showed that diastolic blood pressure is a protective factor of moderate constitution,BMI is a risk factor of phlegm-damp constitution,HDL is the protective factor of phlegm-damp constitution,diastolic blood pressure is a risk factor of damp-heat constitution,systolic blood pressure and LDL are risk factors of blood-stasis constitution,BMI and diastolic blood pressure are protective factors of blood-stasis constitution.Conclusions:phlegm-damp,damp-heat,blood-stasis are main biased constitutions of patients with metabolic syndrome;to prompt:HDL,LDL,BMI,diastolic blood pressure,systolic blood pressure etc.indexes are closely related to the improvement of constitution of patients with MS and the recovery of healthful physiological state.

title: Identification of Metabolic Syndrome Based on Qi Activity of Yinyang As-cending and Descending,Sanjiao and Zangfu from Qi,Blood and Body Fluids
abstract: Metabolic syndrome is characterized as obesity,hyperglycemia(diabetes or impaired glu-cose regulation),dyslipidemia and hypertension. At present,the morbidity of it is increasing. The modern medicine believes that metabolic syndrome leads to many serious complications of the heart,brain and kid-ney. It seriously affects the health. Currently,the advantage of TCM has been drawn the attention. TCM thinks that metabolic syndrome is commonly seen in harmful fluid,overweight,xiaoke,dizziness and vertigo. From the ancient time to the modern era,the pattern/ syndrome differentiation of disorder according to qi,blood and body fluids,sanjiao and zangfu are adopted for the treatment. Yin,yang,qi,blood and body fluid are consid-ered. The location of the disease is in the spleen,liver and kidney. The deficiency of the spleen and kidney is the root cause,while,phlegm,stasis and harmful fluid are the secondary cause. In the paper,based on TCM philosophic thought,metabolic syndrome was identified in view of qi activity of yinyang ascending and de-scending,sanjiao and zangfu with qi,blood and body fluids theories involved so as to run through the methods for the pattern/ syndrome differentiation and collect the thoughts of different schools.

title: Exploring the biological basis for the identification of different syndromes in ischemic heart failure based on joint multi-omics analysis
abstract: Background IHF is a major chronic disease that seriously threatens human health. Qi deficiency and blood stasis syndrome (QDBS), Yang deficiency with blood stasis syndrome (YDBS) and Yang deficiency and blood stasis with fluid retention syndrome (YDBSFR) are the basic syndromes of IHF in Chinese medicine. This study aims to explore the biological basis of the three IHF syndromes through integrated multi-omics research.Methods We analyzed and integrated transcriptomic, proteomic, and targeted metabolomic data from IHF patients and healthy persons to obtain the key biomarkers and enriched pathways of QDBS, YDBS and YDBSFR(Registration No.: ChiCTR2200058314). These biomarkers were combined with clinical indicators to construct the "Disease-Syndromes-Clinical phenotypes-Biomarkers-Pathways" network, and the obtained differential genes and proteins were externally validated.Results The potential biomarkers for QDBS included SDHD, IL10, ACTG1, VWF, MDH2, COX5A, Valeric acid, Succinic Acid and L-Histidine, which were predominantly enriched in TCA cycle, oxidative phosphorylation, platelet activation, and neutrophil extracellular trap formation pathways, demonstrating associations with energy metabolism, coagulation system, and immune-inflammatory responses.YDBS potential biomarkers included TSHR, PRKG1, ATP1A2, GNAI2, APOA2, PLTP, 3-Hydroxybutyrate, Hexadecanoic acid and Palmitelaidic acid, and the combined pathways were mainly enriched in thyroid hormone synthesis, regulation of lipolysis in adipocytes, cholesterol metabolism and PPAR signaling pathways, correlating with hormonal regulation and lipid metabolism. The potential biomarkers of YDBSFR were CNGB1, KCNMA1, PIK3R2, HSPA8, C3, FH, Oxamic acid, N-Acetyl-L-alanine, 4-Hydroxyhippuric acid, and the combined pathways were mainly enriched in aldosterone-regulated sodium reabsorption, cGMP-PKG signaling pathway, neutrophil extracellular trap formation and TCA cycle signaling pathways, which are related to hormone regulation, signal transduction, immune-inflammatory response and energy metabolism. Platelet activation was involved in the whole process of IHF. External validation demonstrated the above core targets.Conclusion This study investigated the biological basis of QDBS, YDBS and YDBSFR in IHF from a modern biomedical perspective, providing references for the objective research of TCM syndrome differentiation.

title: [Traditional Chinese Medicine Perspective on the Pathogenesis and Management of Metabolic Syndrome].
abstract: Metabolic syndrome (MetS) is a cluster of metabolic disorders with diverse clinical manifestations. Jingui Yaolue, or , discusses over 40 different diseases in total. This study reveals that some of these diseases, including tanyin (phlegm-dampness), xulao (consumptive disease), and xiaoke (wasting and thirst), exhibit clinical manifestations similar to those of MetS. Furthermore, there are also correspondences between these diseases and MetS in terms of the etiology and pathogenesis. For example, the pathogenesis involves multiple organs, but is centered on the spleen; the disease arises from deficiency and ultimately leads to a dual deficiency of yin and yang; the syndrome patterns are complex and predominantly characterized by phlegm-turbidity. This article summarizes targeted treatment approaches for MetS mentioned in , including focusing on the spleen in the treatment of diseases involving multiple organs, supporting the vital energy while dealing with excess syndrome, balancing yin and yang in a state of dual deficiency of both yin and yang, and treating phlegm and blood stasis simultaneously due to the close connection between body fluids and blood. Integrating these treatment principles into current traditional Chinese medicine treatment plans for MetS may help optimize traditional Chinese medicine treatment protocols for MetS and improve clinical outcomes.

title: Discussion on Pathogenesis of Metabolic Syndrome Based on the Theory of Yin and Yang in Inner Canon of Huangdi
abstract: Metabolic syndrome (MS) is a common concern of global scholars. Due to its complex pathophysiological characteristics, it has become a common disease of endocrine and cardiovascular diseases. With the change of modern lifestyle, the incidence of MS has increased year by year. MS has a long history and various clinical manifestations. Modern medicine lacks the overall cognition of the dynamic evolution of the course of disease, and only passive symptomatic treatment is currently adopted. MS is a traditional Chinese medicine"Pidan"category, Inner Canon of Huangdi said:"the separation of Yin and Yang, the spirit of it", in the books emphasize disease is the imbalance of Yin and Yang, MS is no exception, is caused by the imbalance of Yin and Yang, Qi and blood, and the imbalance of homeostasis agree without prior without previous consultation on pathological mechanism of modern medicine. This article focuses on the balance of Qi, Yin and Yang in Zangfu organs, and the effect is remarkable. In this paper, from the theory of internal and external Yin and Yang, comparative study of Chinese and Western medicine on MS diagnosis and treatment, in order to provide new ideas for clinical treatment.

title: Investigation of Constitution Types in Patients with Impaired Glucose Tolerance
abstract: Objective:To investigate the constitution types and the syndrome patterns in patients with imparied glusose tolerance(IGT).Methods:A retrospective study was carried out in 251 IGT outpatients and inpatients.The case information of all of the subjects was sorted and analyzed for the identification of constitution types and syndrome patterns.Results:The patients were classified into 4 constitution types.Of 251 cases,patients with the constitution of Qi deficiency and phlegm - dampness accounted for 41.4%,Qi stagnation and damp heat accounted for 27.9%,deficiency of Qi and yin accounted for 16.7%,and yang deficiency and blood stasis accounted for 13.9%.The incidence of IGT combined with IGF was 33.3%in the constitution of Qi and yin deficiency,37.1%in the constitution of yang deficiency and blood stasis, the difference being significant compared with that in the other two types(P<0.05). TC and TG levels were higher in the above four constitution types than that in the normal group(P<0.05),but the difference of FBG and OGTT2hBG was insignificant(P>0.05).Conclusion:Qi deficiency and phlegm -dampness,and Qi stagnation and damp heat are the dominated constitution types of IGT patients,and deficiency of Qi and yin,and yang deficiency and blood stasis come next.All of the above four constitution types have abnormal blood lipid levels.IGT patients with deficiency of Qi and yin,and yang deficiency and blood stasis are apt to have diabetes mellitus.

title: Exploration of Etiology and Pathogenesis of Metabolism Syndrome
abstract: For the knowledge deficiency of pathogenesis and treatment methods for metabolism syndrome in western medicine,and for the lack of systematic and comprehensive acquirement of metabolism syndrome in traditional Chinese medicine,we probed into the traditional Chinese medical etiology and pathogenesis of metabolism syndrome in this study.It is suggested that fatty diet and lack of movement are the exogenous factors,and insufficiency of Zang-fu organs is the endogenous factor for metabolism syndrome.The pathogenesis of metabolism syndrome is due to impairment of spleen and stomach,Qi deficiency and accumulation of phlegm and dampness,and also involves liver-Qi stagnation and retention of phlegm and blood-stasis.In general,the pathogenesis of metabolism syndrome is characterized as deficiency in origin and excess in superficiality,and frequent occurrence of deteriorated case.

title: Prevention and treatment of metabolic syndrome with integrated traditional Chinese and Western medicine
abstract: Metabolic syndrome also can be named insulin resistance syndrome. The main clinical manifestations include metabolic disorders of glucose and lipid and some diseases caused by the metabolic disorder, such as impaired glucose tolerance or diabetes, obesity, hyperlipemia, fatty liver, hypertension, coronary heart disease, microalbuminuria, etc. According to the theory of zang-fu organs (viscera) in traditional Chinese medicine, these diseases all result from the deficiency of spleen-qi. They are characterized by deficiency in the Ben (root) and excess in the Biao (branch). The Ben (root) is the failure of the spleen in transportation, and the Biao (branch) is stagnation of qi, blood, phlegm, fire, dampness and food. In the prevention and treatment of metabolic syndrome, it is advocated that the intervention of medicine should be used as early as possible, so as to slow down the occurrence and development of insulin resistance, and that emphasis should be transferred from decreasing blood glucose alone to comprehensive prevention of risk factors, especially to the prevention of cardiovascular events. The effect of traditional Chinese herbs is not as good as the western drugs in decreasing the blood pressure and glucose. However, the traditional Chinese herbs have distinctive superiority in ameliorating the insulin resistance, protecting the injury of vascular endothelial cells, regulating the metabolism of lipid, inhibiting the hypercoagulability, and treating the inflammation. Moreover, they are relatively safe. Therefore, the integration of the traditional Chinese medicine and western medicine is worth further research.
reason:
